lane_0_pressure.reason: Lark runtime loads tools but API call fails with missing access token (99991661); token infrastructure broken — diagnose storage chain before retry.

token_acquisition_paths.description: Uses SDK internal tenant_access_token manager; no OAuth needed; appId+appSecret sufficient
token_acquisition_paths.fallback_on_failure: none — SDK fails to call API if token fetch fails

token_acquisition_paths.description: Requires valid user access token (from OAuth login or pre-existing token env)

token_acquisition_paths.fallback_on_failure: SDK calls API without token; API returns 99991661

lane_2_token_requirement_mapping.answer_app_secret_sufficient: Yes for tenant_access_token flow; SDK's internal token manager uses appId+appSecret to fetch tenant token automatically
lane_2_token_requirement_mapping.answer_user_vs_app_token: lark_im_v1_chat_list works with both user_access_token and tenant_access_token; SDK auto-selects based on tokenMode
lane_2_token_requirement_mapping.answer_oauth_required: OAuth (interactive browser login) is NOT strictly required if using tenant_access_token mode

lane_3_keytar_analysis.keytar_failure_causes: StorageManager.encrypt/decrypt fails; isInitializedStorageSuccess=false; saveStorageData() returns early without writing; loadStorageData() returns {}
lane_3_keytar_analysis.keytar_failure_does_not_block: tenant_access_token mode (SDK fetches token per-request via HTTP, no storage needed)
lane_3_keytar_analysis.risk: high (blocks persistent OAuth token; does not block tenant_access_token flow)

lane_4_stdio_lifecycle.lark_runtime_probe_attempted: in R147A via get_cached_mcp_tools() which starts stdio process and calls list_tools
lane_4_stdio_lifecycle.tool_list_in_r147a: started stdio process, called list_tools — SDK initialized
lane_4_stdio_lifecycle.tool_call_in_r147c: new stdio process started via get_cached_mcp_tools(); SDK re-initialized; OAuth token not available
lane_4_stdio_lifecycle.explains_missing_access_token: partially — in auto mode with oauth=false, SDK should use tenant_access_token (no storage needed); but SDK tried user token path instead
lane_4_stdio_lifecycle.keytar_fails_breaks: OAuth user token storage only; does not break tenant_access_token flow which uses HTTP fetch per-request

lane_6_repair_options.name: Fix keytar native dependency
lane_6_repair_options.description: Rebuild/reinstall keytar so StorageManager can persist user tokens across stdio process restarts
lane_6_repair_options.reason: Solves root cause of token storage failure, but introduces build complexity. Not the lowest-risk path.

lane_6_repair_options.name: Provide explicit user_access_token via env
lane_6_repair_options.description: Pass USER_ACCESS_TOKEN env var to Lark MCP so SDK skips OAuth flow
lane_6_repair_options.user_action_required: YES — user must obtain a valid Lark user access token via lark-mcp login
lane_6_repair_options.reason: Introduces long-lived secret token as env var — security risk; requires OAuth login which needs keytar to work

lane_6_repair_options.name: Switch to tenant_access_token mode
lane_6_repair_options.description: Pass LARK_TOKEN_MODE=tenant_access_token so SDK uses app-level token fetched via appId+appSecret (no OAuth, no keytar needed)
lane_6_repair_options.reason: Lowest-risk path: no new dependencies, no new secrets, no user interaction. SDK fetches tenant_access_token per-request via HTTP using appId+appSecret.

lane_6_repair_options.name: Enable --oauth flag with interactive login
lane_6_repair_options.description: Add --oauth to lark MCP args; run lark-mcp login first; token stored via keytar (which is broken)
lane_6_repair_options.user_action_required: YES — interactive browser login; keytar must work for persistent token
lane_6_repair_options.reason: Interactive flow blocks automation; keytar still broken so login would not persist

lane_6_repair_options.name: Keep Lark MCP disabled
lane_6_repair_options.description: Disable Lark MCP; focus on Tavily re-enablement or mainline work
lane_6_repair_options.reason: Defers Lark work indefinitely; Option C is a simple fix worth trying first

lane_7_recommended_strategy.recommended_strategy: Option C — Switch to tenant_access_token mode
lane_7_recommended_strategy.reason: LARK_TOKEN_MODE=tenant_access_token makes SDK use app-level tenant_access_token fetched directly via appId+appSecret (no OAuth, no keytar). The SDK's Node.js Client fetches tenant token via /open-apis/auth/v3/tenant_access_token/internal on each request — no persistent storage needed. Lowest-risk path: no new dependencies, no new secrets, no user interaction. Only difference vs user token: some app permissions that require user context may not work, but lark_im_v1_chat_list only needs bot membership which tenant token covers.

validation_plan: 1. Patch extensions_config.json: add env.LARK_TOKEN_MODE=tenant_access_token to lark entry
5. Expect: [] (bot not in any groups) or list of chat objects — both are success
6. If error 99991661: tenant token also failed — fall back to R147F (keytar fix or Lark disable)
7. If error other: inspect and classify
8. Record: tool execution success/failure, error code, response shape, elapsed time
lane_8_r147e_authorization_package.rollback_plan: Remove LARK_TOKEN_MODE from extensions_config.json env field

lane_9_mcp_branch_decision.rationale: R147D identified Option C (tenant_access_token mode) as a viable no-dependency fix — a single env var change. R147E should attempt this before concluding Lark is blocked. Only if R147E fails should Lark be paused and Tavily re-enablement investigated. The tenant_access_token flow bypasses keytar entirely since it fetches tokens per-request via HTTP, not from persistent storage.

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: R147C smoke call failed with 99991661 'Missing access token for authorization' — SDK attempted API call without token
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: diagnosed — SDK in auto mode tried user token path; keytar missing breaks OAuth storage; tenant token mode (Option C) bypasses both

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: R147A keytar.node missing warning is actually HIGH blocker — it breaks OAuth user token persistence. But it does NOT block tenant_access_token flow.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: keytar is required for persistent OAuth token store; memory fallback loses tokens on process restart

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: AuthStore+StorageManager chain: keytar failure → isInitializedStorageSuccess=false → saveStorageData() no-ops → tokens not persisted → memory-only → lost on restart
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: token storage chain traced; tenant_access_token mode avoids this chain (no storage needed)

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Lark MCP --oauth mode requires: (1) keytar working, (2) interactive browser login, (3) redirect URI configured. Current environment fails (1).
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: OAuth is NOT the only path — tenant_access_token mode requires only appId+appSecret

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Lark MCP stdio process is started fresh per get_cached_mcp_tools() call; no token persistence between calls; memory store tokens lost on exit
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: stdio process lifecycle confirmed; tenant_access_token flow works with per-call fetch (no persistence needed)

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: R147D recommended Option C: LARK_TOKEN_MODE=tenant_access_token — bypasses OAuth and keytar dependency
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: repair path selected; R147E authorized to patch extensions_config.json and retry smoke

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Lark MCP SDK in tokenMode='auto' (default): uses LarkAuthHandlerLocal.getLocalAccessToken(appId) which requires keytar-persisted token; falls back to no-token if keytar missing
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147D: auto mode does NOT fall back to tenant_access_token automatically; explicit tokenMode=tenant_access_token needed
